title: Pigmented lesion review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

assessment.text: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.
assessment.rationale: The exact melanoma-assessment section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented, and a clinician-completed lesion assessment; it does not create dermoscopic findings or diagnosis.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
source.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
source.setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

assessment.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
assessment.rationale: The exact safety-netting section supports recording clinician-discussed safety net and arranged follow-up only.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

additions.rationale: The exact skin-cancer recognition section supports documenting a changing pigmented lesion, interval change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or site, inflammation, oozing and sensation change without asserting malignancy or generating referral.

additions.final_wording: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
source.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
source.setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

additions.rationale: The exact melanoma-assessment section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented, and a clinician-completed lesion assessment; it does not create dermoscopic findings or diagnosis.

additions.final_wording: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
